tweets.text: On President’s Day we honor leadership that defends our nation and keeps our citizens safe. President Trump’s unwavering commitment to border security - from his first administration’s push to build the wall and enforce our laws, to the historic progress we continue to make today - has reshaped how we protect America for future generations. 🇺🇸
tweets.text_resolved: On President’s Day we honor leadership that defends our nation and keeps our citizens safe. author.description: Official page of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (@CBP) Commissioner.

tweets.text:
When President Trump came back, we didn’t just fix the border, we fortified it. In his first year we delivered the most secure border in American history, right after the worst crisis we’ve ever faced.

That’s leadership. That’s America First in action.

And make no mistake - we’re just getting started.

tweets.text:
Smart Border Wall is an investment! #Wallswork 

Smart Wall = fewer illegal entries = increased safety and tax savings for every American! 

Even during this unnecessary shutdown, wall construction infused with smart technology continues.   Now if Congress would just approve our appropriations, we could pay these CBP personnel that are dedicated to protecting America.

tweets.text: Happy Easter. A day for faith, family, and the values that keep this country strong. And let’s not forget to say a prayer for those on the front lines today - still on duty, keeping America safe.

tweets.text:
11 months. 0 releases of illegal aliens by Border Patrol. This isn’t temporary or an accident. It’s our new normal.

Thanks to @POTUS & @SecMullinDHS, we’re building on what we know works.

author.description: Official page of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (@CBP) Commissioner.

tweets.text: A lot of noise out there, but the facts don’t change: a unified, government-wide effort ended catch and release. For over 11 months, USBP has not released a single illegal alien arrested at entry. BP stations are nearly empty. The results speak for themselves.

author.description: Official page of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (@CBP) Commissioner.

tweets.text:
.@CBP mourns the loss of Border Patrol Agent Sean M. McDonough. 
 
BPA McDonough started his CBP career in 2007. He recently served at the Wellesley Island Border Patrol Station, Buffalo Sector in New York. He also served in Nogales Station and in Houlton Sector. BPA McDonough also served in the U.S. Army, Army National Guard, and as a Military Police Officer. 
 
He is a true hero who selflessly served to protect America. 
 
As a CBP family, we extend our deepest condolences to the family, friends and colleagues of BPA McDonough.
tweets.text_resolved:

author.description: Official page of the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (@CBP) Commissioner.

tweets.text:
Today, we honor the 161 Border Patrol Agents who made the ultimate sacrifice in the line of duty.

For nearly 102 years, Border Patrol Agents have defended our nation against evolving threats while remaining steadfast in the mission to secure America’s borders and protect the American people.

Because of their courage and service, our country is safer and stronger. We will never forget their sacrifice.
